<doc path="typescript/managed-agents/README.md">
# Managed Agents — TypeScript

> **Bindings not shown here:** This README covers the most common managed-agents flows for TypeScript. If you need a class, method, namespace, field, or behavior that isn't shown, WebFetch the TypeScript SDK repo **or the relevant docs page** from `shared/live-sources.md` rather than guess. Do not extrapolate from cURL shapes or another language's SDK.

> **Agents are persistent — create once, reference by ID.** Store the agent ID returned by `agents.create` and pass it to every subsequent `sessions.create`; do not call `agents.create` in the request path. The Anthropic CLI is one convenient way to create agents and environments from version-controlled YAML — its URL is in `shared/live-sources.md`. The examples below show in-code creation for completeness; in production the create call belongs in setup, not in the request path.

## Installation

```bash
npm install @anthropic-ai/sdk
```

## Client Initialization

```typescript
import Anthropic from "@anthropic-ai/sdk";

// Default — resolves credentials from the environment:
// ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, or ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN, or an `ant auth login` profile.
// Prefer this for local dev; don't hardcode a key.
const client = new Anthropic();

// Explicit API key (only when you must inject a specific key)
const client = new Anthropic({ apiKey: "your-api-key" });
```

---

## Create an Environment

```typescript
const environment = await client.beta.environments.create(
  {
    name: "my-dev-env",
    config: {
      type: "cloud",
      networking: { type: "unrestricted" },
    },
  },
);
console.log(environment.id); // env_...
```

---

## Create an Agent (required first step)

> ⚠️ **There is no inline agent config.** `model`/`system`/`tools` live on the agent object, not the session. Always start with `agents.create()` — the session only takes `agent: { type: "agent", id: agent.id }`.

### Minimal

```typescript
// 1. Create the agent (reusable, versioned)
const agent = await client.beta.agents.create(
  {
    name: "Coding Assistant",
    model: "claude-opus-4-8",
    tools: [{ type: "agent_toolset_20260401", default_config: { enabled: true } }],
  },
);

// 2. Start a session
const session = await client.beta.sessions.create(
  {
    agent: { type: "agent", id: agent.id, version: agent.version },
    environment_id: environment.id,
  },
);
console.log(session.id, session.status);
```

### With system prompt and custom tools

```typescript
const agent = await client.beta.agents.create(
  {
    name: "Code Reviewer",
    model: "claude-opus-4-8",
    system: "You are a senior code reviewer.",
    tools: [
      { type: "agent_toolset_20260401", default_config: { enabled: true } },
      {
        type: "custom",
        name: "run_tests",
        description: "Run the test suite",
        input_schema: {
          type: "object",
          properties: {
            test_path: { type: "string", description: "Path to test file" },
          },
          required: ["test_path"],
        },
      },
    ],
  },
);

const session = await client.beta.sessions.create(
  {
    agent: { type: "agent", id: agent.id, version: agent.version },
    environment_id: environment.id,
    title: "Code review session",
    resources: [
      {
        type: "github_repository",
        url: "https://github.com/owner/repo",
        mount_path: "/workspace/repo",
        authorization_token: process.env.GITHUB_TOKEN,
        branch: "main",
      },
    ],
  },
);
```

---

## Send a User Message

```typescript
await client.beta.sessions.events.send(
  session.id,
  {
    events: [
      {
        type: "user.message",
        content: [{ type: "text", text: "Review the auth module" }],
      },
    ],
  },
);
```

> 💡 **Stream-first:** Open the stream *before* (or concurrently with) sending the message. The stream only delivers events that occur after it opens — stream-after-send means early events arrive buffered in one batch. See [Steering Patterns](../../shared/managed-agents-events.md#steering-patterns).

---

## Stream Events (SSE)

```typescript
// Stream-first: open stream and send concurrently
const [events] = await Promise.all([
  collectStream(session.id),
  client.beta.sessions.events.send(
    session.id,
    { events: [{ type: "user.message", content: [{ type: "text", text: "..." }] }] },
  ),
]);

// Standalone stream iteration:
const stream = await client.beta.sessions.events.stream(
  session.id,
);

for await (const event of stream) {
  switch (event.type) {
    case "agent.message":
      for (const block of event.content) {
        if (block.type === "text") {
          process.stdout.write(block.text);
        }
      }
      break;
    case "agent.custom_tool_use":
      // Custom tool invocation — session is now idle
      console.log(`\nCustom tool call: ${event.name}`);
      console.log(`Input: ${JSON.stringify(event.input)}`);
      break;
    case "session.status_idle":
      console.log("\n--- Agent idle ---");
      break;
    case "session.status_terminated":
      console.log("\n--- Session terminated ---");
      break;
  }
}
```

---

## Provide Custom Tool Result

```typescript
await client.beta.sessions.events.send(
  session.id,
  {
    events: [
      {
        type: "user.custom_tool_result",
        custom_tool_use_id: "sevt_abc123",
        content: [{ type: "text", text: "All 42 tests passed." }],
      },
    ],
  },
);
```

---

## Poll Events

```typescript
const events = await client.beta.sessions.events.list(
  session.id,
);
for (const event of events.data) {
  console.log(`${event.type}: ${event.id}`);
}
```

---

## Full Streaming Loop with Custom Tools

```typescript
function runCustomTool(toolName: string, toolInput: unknown): string {
  if (toolName === "run_tests") {
    // Your tool implementation here
    return "All tests passed.";
  }
  return `Unknown tool: ${toolName}`;
}

async function runSession(client: Anthropic, sessionId: string) {
  while (true) {
    const stream = await client.beta.sessions.events.stream(
      sessionId,
    );

    const toolCalls: Anthropic.Beta.Sessions.BetaManagedAgentsAgentCustomToolUseEvent[] = [];

    for await (const event of stream) {
      if (event.type === "agent.message") {
        for (const block of event.content) {
          if (block.type === "text") {
            process.stdout.write(block.text);
          }
        }
      } else if (event.type === "agent.custom_tool_use") {
        toolCalls.push(event);
      } else if (event.type === "session.status_idle") {
        break;
      } else if (event.type === "session.status_terminated") {
        return;
      }
    }

    if (toolCalls.length === 0) break;

    // Process custom tool calls
    const results = toolCalls.map((call) => ({
      type: "user.custom_tool_result" as const,
      custom_tool_use_id: call.id,
      content: [{ type: "text" as const, text: runCustomTool(call.name, call.input) }],
    }));

    await client.beta.sessions.events.send(
      sessionId,
      { events: results },
    );
  }
}
```

---

## Upload a File

```typescript
import fs from "fs";

const file = await client.beta.files.upload({
  file: fs.createReadStream("data.csv"),
  purpose: "agent",
});

// Use in a session
const session = await client.beta.sessions.create(
  {
    agent: { type: "agent", id: agent.id, version: agent.version },
    environment_id: environment.id,
    resources: [{ type: "file", file_id: file.id, mount_path: "/workspace/data.csv" }],
  },
);
```

---

## List and Download Session Files

List files the agent wrote to `/mnt/session/outputs/` during a session, then download them.

```typescript
import fs from "fs";

// List files associated with a session
const files = await client.beta.files.list({
  scope_id: session.id,
  betas: ["managed-agents-2026-04-01"],
});
for (const f of files.data) {
  console.log(f.filename, f.size_bytes);

  // Download and save to disk
  const resp = await client.beta.files.download(f.id);
  const buffer = Buffer.from(await resp.arrayBuffer());
  fs.writeFileSync(f.filename, buffer);
}
```

> 💡 There's a brief indexing lag (~1–3s) between `session.status_idle` and output files appearing in `files.list`. Retry once or twice if the list is empty.

---

## Session Management

```typescript
// Get session details
const session = await client.beta.sessions.retrieve("sesn_011CZxAbc123Def456");
console.log(session.status, session.usage);

// List sessions
const sessions = await client.beta.sessions.list();

// Delete a session
await client.beta.sessions.delete("sesn_011CZxAbc123Def456");

// Archive a session
await client.beta.sessions.archive("sesn_011CZxAbc123Def456");
```

---

## MCP Server Integration

```typescript
// Agent declares MCP server (no auth here — auth goes in a vault)
const agent = await client.beta.agents.create({
  name: "MCP Agent",
  model: "claude-opus-4-8",
  mcp_servers: [
    { type: "url", name: "my-tools", url: "https://my-mcp-server.example.com/sse" },
  ],
  tools: [
    { type: "agent_toolset_20260401", default_config: { enabled: true } },
    { type: "mcp_toolset", mcp_server_name: "my-tools" },
  ],
});

// Session attaches vault(s) containing credentials for those MCP server URLs
const session = await client.beta.sessions.create({
  agent: agent.id,
  environment_id: environment.id,
  vault_ids: [vault.id],
});
```

See `shared/managed-agents-tools.md` §Vaults for creating vaults and adding credentials.
</doc>

## When to Use WebFetch

Use WebFetch to get the latest documentation when:

- User asks for "latest" or "current" information
- Cached data seems incorrect
- User asks about features not covered here

Live documentation URLs are in `shared/live-sources.md`.

## Common Pitfalls

- Don't truncate inputs when passing files or content to the API. If the content is too long to fit in the context window, notify the user and discuss options (chunking, summarization, etc.) rather than silently truncating.
- **Fable 5 / Opus 4.8 / 4.7 thinking:** Adaptive only. `thinking: {type: "enabled", budget_tokens: N}` returns 400 — `budget_tokens` is fully removed (along with `temperature`, `top_p`, `top_k`). Use `thinking: {type: "adaptive"}`. Opus 4.8 inherits this surface from 4.7 with no new breaking changes; Fable 5 adds one — an explicit `thinking: {type: "disabled"}` returns a 400 (accepted on 4.7/4.8); omit the param instead.
- **Opus 4.6 / Sonnet 4.6 thinking:** Use `thinking: {type: "adaptive"}` — do NOT use `budget_tokens` for new 4.6 code (deprecated on both Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6; for gradual migration of existing code, see the transitional escape hatch in `shared/model-migration.md` — note this carve-out does not apply to Fable 5, Opus 4.7 or 4.8). For older models, `budget_tokens` must be less than `max_tokens` (minimum 1024). This will throw an error if you get it wrong.
- **Prefill removed (Fable 5 and the 4.6/4.7/4.8 family):** Assistant message prefills (last-assistant-turn prefills) return a 400 error on Fable 5, Opus 4.6, Opus 4.7, Opus 4.8, and Sonnet 4.6. Use structured outputs (`output_config.format`) or system prompt instructions to control response format instead. (One exception: the fallback-credit prefill claim — when redeeming a credit with `fallback_has_prefill_claim: true`, the server accepts the echoed assistant message; see the migration guide's refusal section.)
- **Fable 5 `refusal` stop reason:** Safety classifiers may decline a request — a successful HTTP 200 with `stop_reason: "refusal"` (pre-output: empty `content`, nothing billed; mid-stream: partial output billed — discard it). Check `stop_reason` before reading `response.content[0]`, or you'll hit index errors on refused requests. To retry on another model, replay the history as-is — other models drop the refused model's thinking blocks from the prompt, unbilled; no stripping needed (and a fallback-credit redemption must echo the refused body exactly anyway, thinking blocks included). Fallbacks are **opt-in** — new `claude-fable-5` code should include the server-side `fallbacks` parameter by default so a refusal doesn't fail the request outright; see the Claude Fable 5 section above.
- **Fable 5 tokenizer:** Same tokenizer as Opus 4.8 — token counts are roughly unchanged when migrating from Opus 4.7/4.8. Coming from Opus 4.6, Sonnet, Haiku, or older, token counts differ (the Opus 4.7 tokenizer uses ~1×–1.35× as many tokens) — re-measure by calling `count_tokens` once with each model and comparing `input_tokens`.
- **Confirm migration scope before editing:** When a user asks to migrate code to a newer Claude model without naming a specific file, directory, or file list, **ask which scope to apply first** — the entire working directory, a specific subdirectory, or a specific set of files. Do not start editing until the user confirms. Imperative phrasings like "migrate my codebase", "move my project to X", "upgrade to Sonnet 4.6", or bare "migrate to Opus 4.8" are **still ambiguous** — they tell you what to do but not where, so ask. Proceed without asking only when the prompt names an exact file, a specific directory, or an explicit file list ("migrate `app.py`", "migrate everything under `services/`", "update `a.py` and `b.py`"). See `shared/model-migration.md` Step 0.
- **`max_tokens` defaults:** Don't lowball `max_tokens` — hitting the cap truncates output mid-thought and requires a retry. For non-streaming requests, default to `~16000` (keeps responses under SDK HTTP timeouts). For streaming requests, default to `~64000` (timeouts aren't a concern, so give the model room). Only go lower when you have a hard reason: classification (`~256`), cost caps, deliberately short outputs, or **`max_tokens: 0`** for cache pre-warming (see `shared/prompt-caching.md` → Pre-warming).
- **128K output tokens:** Fable 5, Opus 4.6, Opus 4.7, and Opus 4.8 support up to 128K `max_tokens`, but the SDKs require streaming for values that large to avoid HTTP timeouts. Use `.stream()` with `.get_final_message()` / `.finalMessage()`.
- **Tool call JSON parsing (Fable 5 and the 4.6/4.7/4.8 family):** Fable 5, Opus 4.6, Opus 4.7, Opus 4.8, and Sonnet 4.6 may produce different JSON string escaping in tool call `input` fields (e.g., Unicode or forward-slash escaping). Always parse tool inputs with `json.loads()` / `JSON.parse()` — never do raw string matching on the serialized input.
- **Structured outputs (all models):** Use `output_config: {format: {...}}` instead of the deprecated `output_format` parameter on `messages.create()`. This is a general API change, not 4.6-specific.
- **Don't reimplement SDK functionality:** The SDK provides high-level helpers — use them instead of building from scratch. Specifically: use `stream.finalMessage()` instead of wrapping `.on()` events in `new Promise()`; use typed exception classes (`Anthropic.RateLimitError`, etc.) instead of string-matching error messages; use SDK types (`Anthropic.MessageParam`, `Anthropic.Tool`, `Anthropic.Message`, etc.) instead of redefining equivalent interfaces.
- **Don't define custom types for SDK data structures:** The SDK exports types for all API objects. Use `Anthropic.MessageParam` for messages, `Anthropic.Tool` for tool definitions, `Anthropic.ToolUseBlock` / `Anthropic.ToolResultBlockParam` for tool results, `Anthropic.Message` for responses. Defining your own `interface ChatMessage { role: string; content: unknown }` duplicates what the SDK already provides and loses type safety.
- **Report and document output:** For tasks that produce reports, documents, or visualizations, the code execution sandbox has `python-docx`, `python-pptx`, `matplotlib`, `pillow`, and `pypdf` pre-installed. Claude can generate formatted files (DOCX, PDF, charts) and return them via the Files API — consider this for "report" or "document" type requests instead of plain stdout text.

## User Request

model ids for claude fable and sonnet, CLI model aliases

4	> commit/PR bodies, and user-facing UI copy).
5	
6	Write so a careful human reader can't tell a machine drafted it. Lead with the claim, use plain
7	words, stop when the point lands.
8	
9	## Sentences
10	
11	- **State facts with `is` / `are` / `has`.** "The lock is a file", not "serves as / stands as /
12	  represents / acts as / functions as a file".
13	- **Assert the positive directly.** Say what a thing is: "The tag marks the released commit." Drop
14	  the "not X, but Y" and "not just X, it's Y" framing; keep only the Y.
15	- **Vary sentence length.** Follow a long, qualified sentence with a short one. Uniform mid-length
16	  rhythm is the loudest tell.
17	- **Open with the subject.** Start most sentences on the thing being discussed. Keep a connective
18	  ("so", "but", "then") only when the logic genuinely turns; delete "Moreover", "Furthermore",
19	  "Additionally", "Consequently", "In addition" when the next sentence already follows.
20	- **Finish the participle as its own clause.** Prefer "This refreshes the cache, so reads stay warm."
21	  over a trailing "…, ensuring reads stay warm" or "…, highlighting the importance of X". If the tail
22	  only comments on significance, cut it.
23	
24	## Words
25	
26	- **Reach for the plain word.**
27	
28	  | Instead of                                        | Write                            |
29	  | ------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------- |
30	  | utilize · leverage                                | use                              |
31	  | facilitate · enable (as filler)                   | let · help                       |
32	  | in order to                                       | to                               |
33	  | delve into · dive into                            | cover · examine                  |
34	  | a wide range of · a variety of                    | name them, or "several"          |
35	  | robust · powerful · seamless · rich · vibrant     | say what it does                 |
36	  | crucial · pivotal · vital · essential · key       | say why it matters, or cut it    |
37	  | testament to · underscores · highlights           | state the fact plainly           |
38	
39	- **Repeat the exact term.** Call the same thing by the same name each time. Forced synonyms ("the
40	  card", then "the ticket", then "the story") signal machine variation; consistent terms read human
41	  and search cleanly.
42	- **Keep the neutral register.** Describe with concrete nouns and numbers, not sales adjectives.
43	  "Runs 2 sessions concurrently" beats "boasts impressive parallelism".
44	
45	## Structure
46	
47	- **List the real members, however many.** Two is fine; five is fine. Pad nothing to hit three; a
48	  three-item list is legal only when three real items exist.
49	- **Attribute to a named source, or drop the claim.** Cite the file, the person, the doc. Delete
50	  "experts say", "studies show", "it's widely regarded", "some argue".
51	- **Say the thing without warming up.** Cut "It's important to note that", "It's worth mentioning",
52	  "In today's fast-paced world". Start on the content.
53	- **Stop when the point is made.** No "In conclusion" or "Overall" summary of a short piece, no
54	  bolted-on "Challenges and future prospects" section, no closing line about broader significance.
55	
56	## Formatting
57	
58	- **Write the em-dash out of prose.** The em-dash (`—`) is banned. Recast each one:
59	  - Parenthetical aside → wrap in commas, or parentheses for a true digression.
60	  - Elaboration or a list that follows a lead-in → colon.
61	  - Two independent clauses joined for drama → split into two sentences, or use a semicolon.
62	  - Abrupt "…, and the rest" tail → a fresh sentence.
63	- **Sentence-case headings.** "Release lock", not "The Release Lock Mechanism".
64	- **Bold a term once, at its definition.** Let the surrounding prose carry meaning after that.
65	
66	## The tell test
67

69	up to one? Cut every warm-up. If a sentence could sit unchanged in any other doc, it says nothing;

file.content:
# Writing style: prose & copy

> **Load when:** writing any prose (`.knowledge/` docs, playbooks, README/comments-as-prose,
> commit/PR bodies, and user-facing UI copy).

Write so a careful human reader can't tell a machine drafted it. Lead with the claim, use plain
words, stop when the point lands.

## Sentences

- **State facts with `is` / `are` / `has`.** "The lock is a file", not "serves as / stands as /
  represents / acts as / functions as a file".
- **Assert the positive directly.** Say what a thing is: "The tag marks the released commit." Drop
  the "not X, but Y" and "not just X, it's Y" framing; keep only the Y.
- **Vary sentence length.** Follow a long, qualified sentence with a short one. Uniform mid-length
  rhythm is the loudest tell.
- **Open with the subject.** Start most sentences on the thing being discussed. Keep a connective
  ("so", "but", "then") only when the logic genuinely turns; delete "Moreover", "Furthermore",
  "Additionally", "Consequently", "In addition" when the next sentence already follows.
- **Finish the participle as its own clause.** Prefer "This refreshes the cache, so reads stay warm."
  over a trailing "…, ensuring reads stay warm" or "…, highlighting the importance of X". If the tail
  only comments on significance, cut it.

## Words

- **Reach for the plain word.**

  | Instead of                                        | Write                            |
  | ------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------- |
  | utilize · leverage                                | use                              |
  | facilitate · enable (as filler)                   | let · help                       |
  | in order to                                       | to                               |
  | delve into · dive into                            | cover · examine                  |
  | a wide range of · a variety of                    | name them, or "several"          |
  | robust · powerful · seamless · rich · vibrant     | say what it does                 |
  | crucial · pivotal · vital · essential · key       | say why it matters, or cut it    |
  | testament to · underscores · highlights           | state the fact plainly           |

- **Repeat the exact term.** Call the same thing by the same name each time. Forced synonyms ("the
  card", then "the ticket", then "the story") signal machine variation; consistent terms read human
  and search cleanly.
- **Keep the neutral register.** Describe with concrete nouns and numbers, not sales adjectives.
  "Runs 2 sessions concurrently" beats "boasts impressive parallelism".

## Structure

- **List the real members, however many.** Two is fine; five is fine. Pad nothing to hit three; a
  three-item list is legal only when three real items exist.
- **Attribute to a named source, or drop the claim.** Cite the file, the person, the doc. Delete
  "experts say", "studies show", "it's widely regarded", "some argue".
- **Say the thing without warming up.** Cut "It's important to note that", "It's worth mentioning",
  "In today's fast-paced world". Start on the content.
- **Stop when the point is made.** No "In conclusion" or "Overall" summary of a short piece, no
  bolted-on "Challenges and future prospects" section, no closing line about broader significance.

## Formatting

- **Write the em-dash out of prose.** The em-dash (`—`) is banned. Recast each one:
  - Parenthetical aside → wrap in commas, or parentheses for a true digression.
  - Elaboration or a list that follows a lead-in → colon.
  - Two independent clauses joined for drama → split into two sentences, or use a semicolon.
  - Abrupt "…, and the rest" tail → a fresh sentence.
- **Sentence-case headings.** "Release lock", not "The Release Lock Mechanism".
- **Bold a term once, at its definition.** Let the surrounding prose carry meaning after that.

## The tell test

Before shipping prose, reread the opening sentence of each paragraph: does it make a claim, or warm
up to one? Cut every warm-up. If a sentence could sit unchanged in any other doc, it says nothing;
replace it with the specific fact.

gate: { passed: 2026-07-16T21:49:22Z, brief: 6a0ac2c4d1264bf5, overrides: [] }

# Session runner & kind registry

## Goal

The orchestrator spawns, streams, resumes, and kills a real `claude -p` session for any registered
session kind, with the kind fixing model, tool allowlist, system-prompt injection, and context
policy (`.knowledge/architecture/session-kinds.md`). Session events reach the UI live over WS.

## Approach

- New `src/sessions/` module, shaped like `src/board/`: pure session mechanics, no HTTP. A
  `src/server/` service wires it to the WS channels; `src/worker/` routes expose spawn/message.
- Spawn `claude -p` with `--output-format stream-json --verbose`, `--model` and the reasoning
  effort from the kind (the headless effort mechanism is spike-verified first,
  `.knowledge/architecture/claude-integration.md` §Invocation model), `--allowedTools` from the
  kind, `--append-system-prompt` for the kind's contract, cwd = the managed repo. Parse the
  NDJSON event stream (`system/init`, `assistant`, `user`, `rate_limit_event`, `result`) and
  forward typed events over a new WS channel in `src/shared/channels.ts`. Also pass
  `--include-partial-messages` (re-verify the flag against current docs) and forward its
  `stream_event` deltas, so the UI can render assistant output incrementally (001-03 consumes
  them).
- Capture the session id from `system/init`; later user messages resume with `--resume <id>`.
  Session ids persist in card frontmatter through the board store (epic/story files gain their
  `sessions` entry when a chat first attaches; shaping threads get theirs in 001-04, which first
  teaches the board module `shaping/`).
- Reseed on stale: a resume that exits 1 with `No conversation found` starts a fresh session
  seeded from the card, per the chat context policy. Steering: killing the process and resuming
  with a message that states the interruption.
- The kind registry is data: one table row per kind (model, effort, allowlist, prompt template
  reference, context policy), matching the registry in `session-kinds.md`. Only the chat kinds and the two
  cold kinds this milestone uses need entries wired to behavior.
- Reference mechanics: `spikes/stream-json/`; re-verify flags against current CLI docs first
  (`.knowledge/architecture/claude-integration.md`).

## Blast radius

New `src/sessions/` module (the bulk). Additions to `src/shared/channels.ts`,
`src/server/services/`, and `src/worker/routes/`. `src/board/` gains a frontmatter write for
`sessions` ids. No UI changes beyond whatever debug surface verification needs.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Spawning a `shape`-kind session emits `system/init` carrying that kind's model, the events
      stream over WS as they arrive, and a tool call outside the kind's allowlist is denied
      (surfacing in `result.permission_denials`), never executed.
- [ ] A second message resumes the same session id, and the model demonstrates memory of the
      first turn.
- [ ] Killing a session mid-turn (SIGTERM) and resuming with a steering message continues with
      full memory.
- [ ] A resume against a deleted transcript fails loud and a fresh session starts, seeded from
      the card, with the new id persisted (exercised on a card-attached kind, `refine` on a
      story or `define` on an epic; shaping threads gain id persistence in 001-04).
- [ ] Two kinds spawn with visibly different registry rows (`shape` on Fable read-only,
      `research` on Sonnet) with no per-call configuration.

## Out of scope

- The MCP board tools and `--mcp-config` wiring (001-02).
- Any widget or chat UI (001-03).
- Run-kind compaction, the permission-prompt tool, and hooks (roadmap step 2).
- The queue: sessions here spawn directly on demand (001-06 adds the dispatcher).

## Open questions

None.

content.text:
The Helm loop for story 001-01 is now running end to end. Status so far:

**Groundwork committed on master** (3 commits): the spec updates binding the ready gate to the brief hash, the `src/board` schema support for `gate`/`size`/run brief hashes and the `.helm/board/` path move, and the board seed with the session-foundation epic (7 stories, 001-01 gated Ready).

**Run spawned per Helm's own procedure:**
- Worktree at `~/.helm/worktrees/helm/001-01` on branch `helm/001-01-session-runner`, sparse checkout excluding `.helm/board/`, deps installed and `pnpm check` green (needed a `stack` symlink for the `link:../stack` deps and a `pnpm generate` for the virtual-module types).
- Card flipped to `running` with the run entry recorded: session `541581bf-933b-4fc6-a49a-fa57ddea0a92`, brief hash `6a0ac2c4d1264bf5`, started `2026-07-16T23:04:06Z`.
- The session runs headless `claude -p` on **Fable at high effort** with the brief as prompt, an Auto-style allowlist (file edits, pnpm/git/node/claude/kill — the story needs to spawn and SIGTERM real CLI sessions to verify its criteria), `--strict-mcp-config`, and a run contract appended to the system prompt. Events stream to `~/.helm/logs/helm/001-01/run.ndjson`.

The run will take a while (it's the riskiest story in the milestone: a new `src/sessions/` module plus server/worker wiring, with live CLI verification). I'll be notified when it exits; then comes the cold **review session on Sonnet** grading each acceptance criterion, and finally the usage tally summed from every session's `result` events. I'll report back with the graded checklist and the total draw on your account — the approve/request-changes/discard exit stays yours.

content.content:
1	# Final report: story 001-01 — Session runner & kind registry
2	
3	## What was built
4	
5	Two commits on `helm/001-01-session-runner` (`pnpm check` green, tree clean):
6	
7	- **`08e1eeb feat(sessions): session runner and kind registry`**
8	  - `src/sessions/kinds.ts` — the registry as data: one row per kind (all nine from `session-kinds.md`) with model, effort, context policy, and, on the wired kinds (`init`, `shape`, `define`, `refine`, `research`, `adversary`), the read-only tool allowlist and system-prompt contract. `run`/`review`/`conflict` rows carry their settled model/effort/context but are not spawnable until their mechanics (permission presets, test command, worktrees) land; spawning one throws.
9	  - `src/sessions/runner.ts` — spawns `claude -p` with `--output-format stream-json --verbose --include-partial-messages --model --effort --permission-mode default --allowedTools --append-system-prompt --strict-mcp-config`, parses the NDJSON stream through a tolerant zod boundary, exposes `started` (resolves on `system/init`), `done` (exit outcome with stale detection), and `kill()` (SIGTERM). Strips `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`/`ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN` and the nested-session markers from the child env while keeping `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`.
10	  - `src/sessions/events.ts` / `prompts.ts` — event schemas plus the reseed and steering prompt builders.
11	  - `src/shared/channels.ts` — new `session` WS channel: `event` (every CLI event, tagged `{runId, kind, sessionId}`) and `closed` (`exitCode`, `signal`, `stale`).
12	  - `src/server/services/sessions.ts` — wires runner to WS; tracks live/known/interrupted sessions; reseeds stale card-attached sessions; persists ids.
13	  - `src/worker/routes/session.ts` — `session.spawn` / `session.message` / `session.kill`.
14	  - `src/board/store.ts` — `attachStorySession` / `attachEpicSession` frontmatter writes; the write queue moved from `routes/story.ts` to `src/server/write-queue.ts` so moves and session attaches serialize through one writer.
15	- **`cddddae docs`** — `claude-integration.md` (effort flag verified, partial messages, explicit permission mode), `session-kinds.md` (effort no longer unverified), `api.md` (procedures, channel, error codes), `CLAUDE.md` module map.
16	
17	## Per-criterion verification
18	
19	All five criteria were exercised end-to-end against the real CLI (2.1.177) by a throwaway harness (deleted before commit) that booted the actual node server with a toy managed repo, subscribed to `/ws`, and drove the RPC procedures. Observed output:
20	
21	1. **Shape spawn, model, WS streaming, denial** ✅ — init over WS: `model=claude-fable-5 permissionMode=default`; events streamed as they arrived (21 `stream_event` partials); `permission_denials: [{"tool_name":"Bash","tool_input":{"command":"touch denied.txt"...}}]` and `denied.txt` was never created.
22	2. **Resume with memory** ✅ — `session.message` returned the same id; the model replied `"done"`, the word from turn one.
23	3. **Kill mid-turn + steering** ✅ — SIGTERM mid-generation: `closed: exitCode=143 signal=null`, no `result` event; the steering resume kept the id and the model described the interrupted counting task (and, responding to the auto-prepended steering preamble, noted no partial side effects).
24	4. **Stale reseed with persistence** ✅ — `refine` spawn persisted `sessions: { refine: b3868c27-… }` in story frontmatter; after deleting the transcript, the resume failed loud (`closed` frame with `stale: true`, exit 1) and a fresh session answered *"This story is about shipping a blue widget, carved from blue stone, that spins…"* (seeded from the card), with the new id `907a7d94-…` replacing the old in frontmatter.
25	5. **Two kinds, different registry rows** ✅ — `research` spawn with only `{kind, prompt}` reported `model=claude-sonnet-4-6` vs shape's Fable; bonus check: messaging the research session was rejected with `SESSION_COLD` (always-cold policy).
26	
27	## Decisions taken (no user available)
28	
29	- **Effort mechanism**: current CLI has a first-class `--effort` flag (valid: low/medium/high/xhigh/max; invalid values warn and fall back). Recorded in the knowledge base; the older "settings key?" open question is resolved.
30	- **Explicit `--permission-mode default` on every spawn**: the user's own config defaults headless spawns to `auto`, which would auto-approve tools outside the allowlist. Pinning the mode is what makes the denial criterion hold; documented in `claude-integration.md`.
31	- **Unspawnable rows for `run`/`review`/`conflict`**: their allowlists can't be expressed yet without inventing (presets, repo test command, worktree tools), so the rows exist as data but spawn throws. This matches the brief's "only the chat kinds and the two cold kinds need entries wired to behavior".
32	- **Turn lifecycle contract**: `spawn`/`message` return at `system/init`; the session stays `SESSION_BUSY` until its `closed` WS frame (documented in `api.md`). 001-03's chat UI should treat `closed`, not `result`, as end-of-turn.
33	- **Steering preamble is automatic**: a session whose last process ended without a `result` event is marked interrupted, and the next resume gets the "your turn was interrupted" preamble prepended server-side.
34	- **Write-queue move**: a small refactor beyond the listed blast radius, needed so the new frontmatter writes share the existing serialization instead of a parallel queue.
35	
36	## For a human to verify by hand
37	
38	- Nothing functional is outstanding. Two notes: the local CLI is **2.1.177** while the earlier spike README records 2.1.210 (possibly a downgrade/rollback on this machine); all flags were re-verified against the installed binary. And re-running the harness needs a `helm.config.json` pointing at a scratch repo with one epic/story (I used `/tmp/helm-toy`, left in place).

# Final report: story 001-01 — Session runner & kind registry

## What was built

Two commits on `helm/001-01-session-runner` (`pnpm check` green, tree clean):

- **`08e1eeb feat(sessions): session runner and kind registry`**
  - `src/sessions/kinds.ts` — the registry as data: one row per kind (all nine from `session-kinds.md`) with model, effort, context policy, and, on the wired kinds (`init`, `shape`, `define`, `refine`, `research`, `adversary`), the read-only tool allowlist and system-prompt contract. `run`/`review`/`conflict` rows carry their settled model/effort/context but are not spawnable until their mechanics (permission presets, test command, worktrees) land; spawning one throws.
  - `src/sessions/runner.ts` — spawns `claude -p` with `--output-format stream-json --verbose --include-partial-messages --model --effort --permission-mode default --allowedTools --append-system-prompt --strict-mcp-config`, parses the NDJSON stream through a tolerant zod boundary, exposes `started` (resolves on `system/init`), `done` (exit outcome with stale detection), and `kill()` (SIGTERM). Strips `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`/`ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN` and the nested-session markers from the child env while keeping `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`.
  - `src/sessions/events.ts` / `prompts.ts` — event schemas plus the reseed and steering prompt builders.
  - `src/shared/channels.ts` — new `session` WS channel: `event` (every CLI event, tagged `{runId, kind, sessionId}`) and `closed` (`exitCode`, `signal`, `stale`).
  - `src/server/services/sessions.ts` — wires runner to WS; tracks live/known/interrupted sessions; reseeds stale card-attached sessions; persists ids.
  - `src/worker/routes/session.ts` — `session.spawn` / `session.message` / `session.kill`.
  - `src/board/store.ts` — `attachStorySession` / `attachEpicSession` frontmatter writes; the write queue moved from `routes/story.ts` to `src/server/write-queue.ts` so moves and session attaches serialize through one writer.
- **`cddddae docs`** — `claude-integration.md` (effort flag verified, partial messages, explicit permission mode), `session-kinds.md` (effort no longer unverified), `api.md` (procedures, channel, error codes), `CLAUDE.md` module map.

## Per-criterion verification

All five criteria were exercised end-to-end against the real CLI (2.1.177) by a throwaway harness (deleted before commit) that booted the actual node server with a toy managed repo, subscribed to `/ws`, and drove the RPC procedures. Observed output:

1. **Shape spawn, model, WS streaming, denial** ✅ — init over WS: `model=claude-fable-5 permissionMode=default`; events streamed as they arrived (21 `stream_event` partials); `permission_denials: [{"tool_name":"Bash","tool_input":{"command":"touch denied.txt"...}}]` and `denied.txt` was never created.
2. **Resume with memory** ✅ — `session.message` returned the same id; the model replied `"done"`, the word from turn one.
3. **Kill mid-turn + steering** ✅ — SIGTERM mid-generation: `closed: exitCode=143 signal=null`, no `result` event; the steering resume kept the id and the model described the interrupted counting task (and, responding to the auto-prepended steering preamble, noted no partial side effects).
4. **Stale reseed with persistence** ✅ — `refine` spawn persisted `sessions: { refine: b3868c27-… }` in story frontmatter; after deleting the transcript, the resume failed loud (`closed` frame with `stale: true`, exit 1) and a fresh session answered *"This story is about shipping a blue widget, carved from blue stone, that spins…"* (seeded from the card), with the new id `907a7d94-…` replacing the old in frontmatter.
5. **Two kinds, different registry rows** ✅ — `research` spawn with only `{kind, prompt}` reported `model=claude-sonnet-4-6` vs shape's Fable; bonus check: messaging the research session was rejected with `SESSION_COLD` (always-cold policy).

## Decisions taken (no user available)

- **Effort mechanism**: current CLI has a first-class `--effort` flag (valid: low/medium/high/xhigh/max; invalid values warn and fall back). Recorded in the knowledge base; the older "settings key?" open question is resolved.
- **Explicit `--permission-mode default` on every spawn**: the user's own config defaults headless spawns to `auto`, which would auto-approve tools outside the allowlist. Pinning the mode is what makes the denial criterion hold; documented in `claude-integration.md`.
- **Unspawnable rows for `run`/`review`/`conflict`**: their allowlists can't be expressed yet without inventing (presets, repo test command, worktree tools), so the rows exist as data but spawn throws. This matches the brief's "only the chat kinds and the two cold kinds need entries wired to behavior".
- **Turn lifecycle contract**: `spawn`/`message` return at `system/init`; the session stays `SESSION_BUSY` until its `closed` WS frame (documented in `api.md`). 001-03's chat UI should treat `closed`, not `result`, as end-of-turn.
- **Steering preamble is automatic**: a session whose last process ended without a `result` event is marked interrupted, and the next resume gets the "your turn was interrupted" preamble prepended server-side.
- **Write-queue move**: a small refactor beyond the listed blast radius, needed so the new frontmatter writes share the existing serialization instead of a parallel queue.

## For a human to verify by hand

- Nothing functional is outstanding. Two notes: the local CLI is **2.1.177** while the earlier spike README records 2.1.210 (possibly a downgrade/rollback on this machine); all flags were re-verified against the installed binary. And re-running the harness needs a `helm.config.json` pointing at a scratch repo with one epic/story (I used `/tmp/helm-toy`, left in place).

---

status: running

  - { n: 1, session: 541581bf-933b-4fc6-a49a-fa57ddea0a92, brief: 6a0ac2c4d1264bf5, started: 2026-07-16T23:04:06Z }

# Session runner & kind registry

## Goal

The orchestrator spawns, streams, resumes, and kills a real `claude -p` session for any registered
session kind, with the kind fixing model, tool allowlist, system-prompt injection, and context
policy (`.knowledge/architecture/session-kinds.md`). Session events reach the UI live over WS.

## Approach

- New `src/sessions/` module, shaped like `src/board/`: pure session mechanics, no HTTP. A
  `src/server/` service wires it to the WS channels; `src/worker/` routes expose spawn/message.
- Spawn `claude -p` with `--output-format stream-json --verbose`, `--model` and the reasoning
  effort from the kind (the headless effort mechanism is spike-verified first,
  `.knowledge/architecture/claude-integration.md` §Invocation model), `--allowedTools` from the
  kind, `--append-system-prompt` for the kind's contract, cwd = the managed repo. Parse the
  NDJSON event stream (`system/init`, `assistant`, `user`, `rate_limit_event`, `result`) and
  forward typed events over a new WS channel in `src/shared/channels.ts`. Also pass
  `--include-partial-messages` (re-verify the flag against current docs) and forward its
  `stream_event` deltas, so the UI can render assistant output incrementally (001-03 consumes
  them).
- Capture the session id from `system/init`; later user messages resume with `--resume <id>`.
  Session ids persist in card frontmatter through the board store (epic/story files gain their
  `sessions` entry when a chat first attaches; shaping threads get theirs in 001-04, which first
  teaches the board module `shaping/`).
- Reseed on stale: a resume that exits 1 with `No conversation found` starts a fresh session
  seeded from the card, per the chat context policy. Steering: killing the process and resuming
  with a message that states the interruption.
- The kind registry is data: one table row per kind (model, effort, allowlist, prompt template
  reference, context policy), matching the registry in `session-kinds.md`. Only the chat kinds and the two
  cold kinds this milestone uses need entries wired to behavior.
- Reference mechanics: `spikes/stream-json/`; re-verify flags against current CLI docs first
  (`.knowledge/architecture/claude-integration.md`).

## Blast radius

New `src/sessions/` module (the bulk). Additions to `src/shared/channels.ts`,
`src/server/services/`, and `src/worker/routes/`. `src/board/` gains a frontmatter write for
`sessions` ids. No UI changes beyond whatever debug surface verification needs.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Spawning a `shape`-kind session emits `system/init` carrying that kind's model, the events
      stream over WS as they arrive, and a tool call outside the kind's allowlist is denied
      (surfacing in `result.permission_denials`), never executed.
- [ ] A second message resumes the same session id, and the model demonstrates memory of the
      first turn.
- [ ] Killing a session mid-turn (SIGTERM) and resuming with a steering message continues with
      full memory.
- [ ] A resume against a deleted transcript fails loud and a fresh session starts, seeded from
      the card, with the new id persisted (exercised on a card-attached kind, `refine` on a
      story or `define` on an epic; shaping threads gain id persistence in 001-04).
- [ ] Two kinds spawn with visibly different registry rows (`shape` on Fable read-only,
      `research` on Sonnet) with no per-call configuration.

## Out of scope

- The MCP board tools and `--mcp-config` wiring (001-02).
- Any widget or chat UI (001-03).
- Run-kind compaction, the permission-prompt tool, and hooks (roadmap step 2).
- The queue: sessions here spawn directly on demand (001-06 adds the dispatcher).

## Open questions

None.

---
# Session runner & kind registry

## Goal

The orchestrator spawns, streams, resumes, and kills a real `claude -p` session for any registered
session kind, with the kind fixing model, tool allowlist, system-prompt injection, and context
policy (`.knowledge/architecture/session-kinds.md`). Session events reach the UI live over WS.

## Approach

- New `src/sessions/` module, shaped like `src/board/`: pure session mechanics, no HTTP. A
  `src/server/` service wires it to the WS channels; `src/worker/` routes expose spawn/message.
- Spawn `claude -p` with `--output-format stream-json --verbose`, `--model` and the reasoning
  effort from the kind (the headless effort mechanism is spike-verified first,
  `.knowledge/architecture/claude-integration.md` §Invocation model), `--allowedTools` from the
  kind, `--append-system-prompt` for the kind's contract, cwd = the managed repo. Parse the
  NDJSON event stream (`system/init`, `assistant`, `user`, `rate_limit_event`, `result`) and
  forward typed events over a new WS channel in `src/shared/channels.ts`. Also pass
  `--include-partial-messages` (re-verify the flag against current docs) and forward its
  `stream_event` deltas, so the UI can render assistant output incrementally (001-03 consumes
  them).
- Capture the session id from `system/init`; later user messages resume with `--resume <id>`.
  Session ids persist in card frontmatter through the board store (epic/story files gain their
  `sessions` entry when a chat first attaches; shaping threads get theirs in 001-04, which first
  teaches the board module `shaping/`).
- Reseed on stale: a resume that exits 1 with `No conversation found` starts a fresh session
  seeded from the card, per the chat context policy. Steering: killing the process and resuming
  with a message that states the interruption.
- The kind registry is data: one table row per kind (model, effort, allowlist, prompt template
  reference, context policy), matching the registry in `session-kinds.md`. Only the chat kinds and the two
  cold kinds this milestone uses need entries wired to behavior.
- Reference mechanics: `spikes/stream-json/`; re-verify flags against current CLI docs first
  (`.knowledge/architecture/claude-integration.md`).

## Blast radius

New `src/sessions/` module (the bulk). Additions to `src/shared/channels.ts`,
`src/server/services/`, and `src/worker/routes/`. `src/board/` gains a frontmatter write for
`sessions` ids. No UI changes beyond whatever debug surface verification needs.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Spawning a `shape`-kind session emits `system/init` carrying that kind's model, the events
      stream over WS as they arrive, and a tool call outside the kind's allowlist is denied
      (surfacing in `result.permission_denials`), never executed.
- [ ] A second message resumes the same session id, and the model demonstrates memory of the
      first turn.
- [ ] Killing a session mid-turn (SIGTERM) and resuming with a steering message continues with
      full memory.
- [ ] A resume against a deleted transcript fails loud and a fresh session starts, seeded from
      the card, with the new id persisted (exercised on a card-attached kind, `refine` on a
      story or `define` on an epic; shaping threads gain id persistence in 001-04).
- [ ] Two kinds spawn with visibly different registry rows (`shape` on Fable read-only,
      `research` on Sonnet) with no per-call configuration.

## Out of scope

- The MCP board tools and `--mcp-config` wiring (001-02).
- Any widget or chat UI (001-03).
- Run-kind compaction, the permission-prompt tool, and hooks (roadmap step 2).
- The queue: sessions here spawn directly on demand (001-06 adds the dispatcher).

## Open questions

None.

## Grading: Story 001-01 — Session runner & kind registry

**Type-check result (command run this session):** `pnpm check` — passed cleanly, 38 files, no errors.

---

### AC 1 — `shape`-kind spawn emits `system/init` carrying the kind's model; events stream over WS; tool call outside allowlist denied in `result.permission_denials`

**Verdict:** unclear

**Evidence (code):**
- `src/sessions/runner.ts:88–109` — `spawnSessionProcess` builds args: `--model fable`, `--allowedTools Read,Grep,Glob`, `--permission-mode default`, `--output-format stream-json --verbose`. Every parsed CLI event reaches `options.onEvent`.
- `src/server/services/sessions.ts:152–161` — `runTurn` forwards each event to `handle?.broadcast("event", { runId, kind, sessionId, event })`.
- `src/sessions/events.ts:13–33` — `sessionEventSchema` is a `looseObject` (passthrough). The raw CLI `system/init` payload (including its `model` field) is forwarded verbatim inside the WS `event` envelope. The model is not hoisted to the outer envelope but is present in the inner event.
- `src/sessions/events.ts:10–33` — `parseInitEvent` validates the `system/init` shape strictly; `tools` array from the CLI init event is also surfaced on `SessionInit`.
- Tool denial: the spike README (`spikes/stream-json/README.md`) spike-verifies `permission_denials` exists on `result` events, and the cli-integration doc verifies `--allowedTools` + `--permission-mode default` restricts execution. Whether a model tool-use for an out-of-allowlist tool surfaces specifically in `permission_denials` (not just silently dropped) requires a live run.
- **Unverified risk:** `--model fable` and `--model sonnet` are passed as short lowercase family aliases. The spike (`lib.ts:74`) only verified `--model haiku`; "fable" as a `--model` value is not spike-verified and the claude-integration.md does not record that family aliases resolve correctly. If the CLI requires a full id (`claude-fable-5`), spawning fails before `system/init`.

**[ ]** Human check:
1. Run `session.spawn({ kind: "shape", prompt: "What files are in this repo?" })`.
2. Observe the WS `session` channel `event` message where `event.type === "system"` and `event.subtype === "init"`. Confirm `event.model` matches the Fable model id.
3. In a subsequent message, trigger a tool call not in `["Read", "Grep", "Glob"]` (e.g., a Bash call). Observe the `result` event's `permission_denials` array is non-empty and the tool was not executed.
4. If spawning errors with an unknown-model warning, replace `"fable"` in `kinds.ts:KindRow.model` with the verified full model id.

---

### AC 2 — Second message resumes the same session id; model demonstrates memory of the first turn

**Verdict:** unclear

**Evidence (code):**
- `src/server/services/sessions.ts:62–97` — `messageSession` resolves `SessionInfo` from `known` (in-memory) or `findOnBoard` (from frontmatter), checks the session is not busy or cold-kind, and delegates to `runTurn` with `resume: input.sessionId`.
- `src/sessions/runner.ts:103–104` — when `options.resume !== undefined`, `"--resume", options.resume` is appended to CLI args.
- Spike README: "`--resume` keeps the session id stable across any number of resumes; context carries over."
- Memory demonstration is inherently runtime.

**[ ]** Human check: Spawn a `shape` session with "Name a color." Call `session.message` with that session id and "What color did I just ask about?" Confirm the response references the prior turn.

---

### AC 3 — Killing mid-turn (SIGTERM), then resuming with a steering message continues with full memory

**Verdict:** unclear

**Evidence (code):**
- `src/server/services/sessions.ts:110–118` — `killSession` calls `child.kill()` which sends SIGTERM.
- `src/server/services/sessions.ts:162–175` — the `done.then` handler: when no `result` event was seen and the outcome is not stale, `interrupted.add(sessionId)`.
- `src/server/services/sessions.ts:83–87` — `messageSession` detects `interrupted.has` and prepends `steeringPrompt(message)` (`src/sessions/prompts.ts:21–28`).
- `src/sessions/prompts.ts:21–28` — steering message states "Your previous turn was interrupted mid-run; a tool call in flight may have partially applied its side effects."
- Spike README: "Kill mid-tool-call is safe to resume: same session id, full memory, but the resumed model believes the interrupted tool call never ran."
- **Ordering risk:** `killSession` returns synchronously after sending SIGTERM; `live.delete` and `interrupted.add` happen in the async `done.then` handler. If the client calls `messageSession` before the process fully closes and `done.then` runs, it hits `SESSION_BUSY` (409). The protocol requires the client to wait for the WS `closed` event before calling message. This is not enforced at the API layer — it is a client contract, not server enforcement.

**[ ]** Human check: Spawn a turn with a multi-step task. Call `session.kill`. Wait for the WS `closed` event. Then call `session.message` with a steering instruction. Confirm the response demonstrates full memory of the prior incomplete turn and acknowledges the interruption.

---

### AC 4 — Resume against deleted transcript fails loud; fresh session starts seeded from card; new id persisted (exercised on `refine`/`define`)

**Verdict:** unclear

**Evidence (code):**
- `src/sessions/runner.ts:59` — `STALE_STDERR = "No conversation found with session ID"` exactly matches the spike-verified stderr string (README: "exit 1, stderr `No conversation found with session ID: …`").
- `src/sessions/runner.ts:136–143` — `outcome.stale = exitCode === 1 && stderr.includes(STALE_STDERR)`; if `init === undefined`, rejects `started` with `SessionSpawnError { stale: true }`.
- `src/server/services/sessions.ts:78–102` — `messageSession` catch block: if `error.stale && info.attach !== undefined`, clears old id from `known`/`interrupted`, reads card raw, calls `runTurn` with `reseedPrompt(raw, message)` (no `resume`).
- `src/sessions/prompts.ts:5–16` — `reseedPrompt` wraps card content in `<card>` tags with a preamble stating the transcript expired.
- `src/server/services/sessions.ts:185–191` — `runTurn` calls `persistAttach(attach, init.sessionId)` when `attach !== undefined && options.resume !== init.sessionId` (always true on reseed since `options.resume` is `undefined`).
- `src/board/store.ts:369–402` — `attachStorySession` / `attachEpicSession` do read-modify-write through the write queue.
- WS "loud fail": `void child.done.then` in `runTurn` broadcasts `{ stale: true }` on the `closed` message even while `started` is rejecting — the broadcast is not conditional on the catch block in `messageSession`.
- `src/server/services/sessions.ts:239–252` — `resolveAttach`: only `refine` (story) and `define` (epic) get an `Attach`; cold-only kinds without attach correctly throw `SESSION_STALE` (410) instead of reseeding.

**[ ]** Human check:
1. Spawn a `refine` session on a story. Note the session id. Manually delete `~/.claude/projects/<slug>/<session-id>.jsonl`. Call `session.message` with that id. Confirm: (a) WS emits `closed` with `stale: true`, (b) the API returns a new session id, (c) the story's frontmatter `sessions.refine` is updated to the new id.

---

### AC 5 — Two kinds spawn with visibly different registry rows (`shape` Fable read-only, `research` Sonnet); no per-call configuration

**Verdict:** unclear

**Evidence (code):**
- `src/sessions/kinds.ts:47–68` — `KIND_REGISTRY.shape = { model: "fable", effort: "high", context: "reseed-on-stale", tools: ["Read","Grep","Glob"], systemPrompt: "...shaping chat..." }`.
- `src/sessions/kinds.ts:69–75` — `KIND_REGISTRY.research = { model: "sonnet", effort: "high", context: "always-cold", tools: ["Read","Grep","Glob"], systemPrompt: "...research session..." }`.
- `src/worker/routes/session.ts:12–27` — `spawnInputSchema` accepts only `kind/storyId/epicId/prompt`; no model or tool override path exists.
- `src/sessions/runner.ts:81–109` — `spawnableRow(kind)` drives all CLI flags from the registry row; callers supply only `kind/cwd/prompt/resume`.
- `pnpm check` passed — types confirmed correct.
- **Unverified risk (same as AC1):** `--model fable` and `--model sonnet` as CLI values are not spike-verified. The spike used `--model haiku`. If the CLI rejects the family alias "fable", both kinds fail to spawn.

**[ ]** Human check:
1. Spawn `shape` and `research` sessions in sequence.
2. On each `system/init` WS event, confirm `event.model` reports different model ids (one Fable, one Sonnet).
3. If either spawn fails on model name, verify the valid `--model` alias via `claude --help` or docs, and update `KindRow.model` type + registry to use full model ids.

---

### Blast radius

Declared scope: `src/sessions/` (new), additions to `src/shared/channels.ts`, `src/server/services/`, `src/worker/routes/`, `src/board/` frontmatter writes.

**All touched files are within declared scope.** Two small observations:

- `src/server/write-queue.ts` is a **new file** not named in the blast radius. It is a refactor: the identical implementation was already in `src/worker/routes/story.ts` (lines confirmed in diff) and was promoted to a shared module so sessions can also serialize writes. This is correct hygiene, not scope creep.
- `src/board/schema.ts:21–23` gains `epicIdSchema`, not mentioned in the blast radius. Required by the session route to validate `epicId`. Minimal, appropriate addition.

No UI changes, no test files, no new external dependencies.

---

### Out of scope

- MCP board tools / `--mcp-config` wiring: not present ✓
- Widget or chat UI: not present ✓
- Run-kind compaction, permission-prompt tool, hooks: not present ✓
- Queue dispatcher: sessions spawn directly (`spawnSession` is synchronous from the caller's view) ✓

# Session runner & kind registry

## Goal

The orchestrator spawns, streams, resumes, and kills a real `claude -p` session for any registered
session kind, with the kind fixing model, tool allowlist, system-prompt injection, and context
policy (`.knowledge/architecture/session-kinds.md`). Session events reach the UI live over WS.

## Approach

- New `src/sessions/` module, shaped like `src/board/`: pure session mechanics, no HTTP. A
  `src/server/` service wires it to the WS channels; `src/worker/` routes expose spawn/message.
- Spawn `claude -p` with `--output-format stream-json --verbose`, `--model` and the reasoning
  effort from the kind (the headless effort mechanism is spike-verified first,
  `.knowledge/architecture/claude-integration.md` §Invocation model), `--allowedTools` from the
  kind, `--append-system-prompt` for the kind's contract, cwd = the managed repo. Parse the
  NDJSON event stream (`system/init`, `assistant`, `user`, `rate_limit_event`, `result`) and
  forward typed events over a new WS channel in `src/shared/channels.ts`. Also pass
  `--include-partial-messages` (re-verify the flag against current docs) and forward its
  `stream_event` deltas, so the UI can render assistant output incrementally (001-03 consumes
  them).
- Capture the session id from `system/init`; later user messages resume with `--resume <id>`.
  Session ids persist in card frontmatter through the board store (epic/story files gain their
  `sessions` entry when a chat first attaches; shaping threads get theirs in 001-04, which first
  teaches the board module `shaping/`).
- Reseed on stale: a resume that exits 1 with `No conversation found` starts a fresh session
  seeded from the card, per the chat context policy. Steering: killing the process and resuming
  with a message that states the interruption.
- The kind registry is data: one table row per kind (model, effort, allowlist, prompt template
  reference, context policy), matching the registry in `session-kinds.md`. Only the chat kinds and the two
  cold kinds this milestone uses need entries wired to behavior.
- Reference mechanics: `spikes/stream-json/`; re-verify flags against current CLI docs first
  (`.knowledge/architecture/claude-integration.md`).

## Blast radius

New `src/sessions/` module (the bulk). Additions to `src/shared/channels.ts`,
`src/server/services/`, and `src/worker/routes/`. `src/board/` gains a frontmatter write for
`sessions` ids. No UI changes beyond whatever debug surface verification needs.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Spawning a `shape`-kind session emits `system/init` carrying that kind's model, the events
      stream over WS as they arrive, and a tool call outside the kind's allowlist is denied
      (surfacing in `result.permission_denials`), never executed.
- [ ] A second message resumes the same session id, and the model demonstrates memory of the
      first turn.
- [ ] Killing a session mid-turn (SIGTERM) and resuming with a steering message continues with
      full memory.
- [ ] A resume against a deleted transcript fails loud and a fresh session starts, seeded from
      the card, with the new id persisted (exercised on a card-attached kind, `refine` on a
      story or `define` on an epic; shaping threads gain id persistence in 001-04).
- [ ] Two kinds spawn with visibly different registry rows (`shape` on Fable read-only,
      `research` on Sonnet) with no per-call configuration.

## Out of scope

- The MCP board tools and `--mcp-config` wiring (001-02).
- Any widget or chat UI (001-03).
- Run-kind compaction, the permission-prompt tool, and hooks (roadmap step 2).
- The queue: sessions here spawn directly on demand (001-06 adds the dispatcher).

## Open questions

None.

content.text:
The full Helm loop ran end to end on story **001-01 (Session runner & kind registry)**, and the card now sits in **Review** waiting for your exit. Total draw from your account across every spawned session: **~420k fresh input tokens, ~113k output tokens, ~8.25M cache reads — about $18.9 in modeled API cost** (on your Max subscription this is pool draw, not billing). Breakdown at the bottom.

## What the run built

Two commits on `helm/001-01-session-runner` (21.5 min, 109 turns, `pnpm check` green, zero permission denials):

- `src/sessions/` — the kind registry as data (all nine kinds; `run`/`review`/`conflict` rows exist but throw on spawn until their mechanics land), the `claude -p` runner (spawn/stream/resume/kill, stale detection, env scrubbing), event schemas, and the reseed/steering prompt builders.
- `src/server/services/sessions.ts` + a new `session` WS channel, `src/worker/routes/session.ts` (`spawn`/`message`/`kill`), and `attachStorySession`/`attachEpicSession` frontmatter writes in `src/board/` (the write queue moved to `src/server/write-queue.ts` so all board writes share one serializer — a small refactor beyond the declared blast radius, flagged and justified).
- Docs updated: the `--effort` flag is now verified fact (the CLI has it first-class), plus API and module-map updates.

The run verified all five acceptance criteria **live against the real CLI** with a throwaway harness on a toy repo: it observed the Fable/Sonnet models in `system/init`, the denial in `permission_denials`, resume memory, SIGTERM + steering recovery, and the stale-transcript reseed with the new id persisted to frontmatter.

## Review (two cold Sonnet sessions, per the spec)

**Spec axis — `TALLY: 0/5 met, 5/5 unclear, 0 not met`.** This is the expected cold-reader result, not a red flag: the reviewer has read-only tools and can't spawn sessions, so every runtime criterion stays unchecked with hand-verification steps. Its code-path tracing found no contradictions with the brief. Its one substantive risk — `--model fable`/`sonnet` short aliases being unverified — is already answered by the run's live evidence (`system/init` reported `claude-fable-5` and `claude-sonnet-4-6`). It also noted a real client-contract subtlety: after `kill`, callers must wait for the WS `closed` frame before resuming, which the server doesn't enforce (relevant for 001-03). Blast radius and out-of-scope both check out clean.

**Standards axis — `VERDICT: 0 blockers, 2 should-fix, 5 nits.`** All small: a task-reference in a comment, em-dashes in two `api.md` bullets, and four comment-policy nits. Per the procedure these gate approve until each is accepted (queued for a follow-up) or dismissed with a reason.

## Usage tally (summed from every session's result events + transcripts)

| Session | Model | Fresh input (incl. cache writes) | Output | Cache reads | Modeled cost |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Implementation run | Fable, high | 265,633 | 82,332 | 7,393,514 | $16.75 |
| Nested verification spawns (4) | Fable + Sonnet | 50,007 | 929 | 263,452 | ~$0.90 |
| Review: spec | Sonnet, high | 59,075 | 14,906 | 427,868 | $0.71 |
| Review: standards | Sonnet, high | 44,774 | 14,632 | 164,033 | $0.54 |
| **Total** | | **~419,500** | **~112,800** | **~8,250,000** | **~$18.9** |

Not counted: this orchestration session itself (I can't read my own usage from inside), which shares the same 5-hour pool. The `rate_limit_event` on spawn reported `allowed` with the window resetting at epoch 1784250000.

## Your exit (the human decides every one)

- **Approve** — I merge `helm/001-01-session-runner` to master, delete worktree + branch, card → Done. The 2 should-fix/5 nits would need accepting-into-a-follow-up or dismissal first.
- **Request changes** — your comments (e.g. "apply the 7 standards findings") resume the same session in the same worktree at xhigh effort; card → Running.
- **Discard** — worktree + branch deleted, card → Ready, run history kept.

Branch base still matches master HEAD, so no rebase is needed. Full artifacts are under `~/.helm/logs/helm/001-01/` (run stream, both review reports, the run's final report).

# Session runner & kind registry

## Goal

The orchestrator spawns, streams, resumes, and kills a real `claude -p` session for any registered
session kind, with the kind fixing model, tool allowlist, system-prompt injection, and context
policy (`.knowledge/architecture/session-kinds.md`). Session events reach the UI live over WS.

## Approach

- New `src/sessions/` module, shaped like `src/board/`: pure session mechanics, no HTTP. A
  `src/server/` service wires it to the WS channels; `src/worker/` routes expose spawn/message.
- Spawn `claude -p` with `--output-format stream-json --verbose`, `--model` and the reasoning
  effort from the kind (the headless effort mechanism is spike-verified first,
  `.knowledge/architecture/claude-integration.md` §Invocation model), `--allowedTools` from the
  kind, `--append-system-prompt` for the kind's contract, cwd = the managed repo. Parse the
  NDJSON event stream (`system/init`, `assistant`, `user`, `rate_limit_event`, `result`) and
  forward typed events over a new WS channel in `src/shared/channels.ts`. Also pass
  `--include-partial-messages` (re-verify the flag against current docs) and forward its
  `stream_event` deltas, so the UI can render assistant output incrementally (001-03 consumes
  them).
- Capture the session id from `system/init`; later user messages resume with `--resume <id>`.
  Session ids persist in card frontmatter through the board store (epic/story files gain their
  `sessions` entry when a chat first attaches; shaping threads get theirs in 001-04, which first
  teaches the board module `shaping/`).
- Reseed on stale: a resume that exits 1 with `No conversation found` starts a fresh session
  seeded from the card, per the chat context policy. Steering: killing the process and resuming
  with a message that states the interruption.
- The kind registry is data: one table row per kind (model, effort, allowlist, prompt template
  reference, context policy), matching the registry in `session-kinds.md`. Only the chat kinds and the two
  cold kinds this milestone uses need entries wired to behavior.
- Reference mechanics: `spikes/stream-json/`; re-verify flags against current CLI docs first
  (`.knowledge/architecture/claude-integration.md`).

## Blast radius

New `src/sessions/` module (the bulk). Additions to `src/shared/channels.ts`,
`src/server/services/`, and `src/worker/routes/`. `src/board/` gains a frontmatter write for
`sessions` ids. No UI changes beyond whatever debug surface verification needs.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Spawning a `shape`-kind session emits `system/init` carrying that kind's model, the events
      stream over WS as they arrive, and a tool call outside the kind's allowlist is denied
      (surfacing in `result.permission_denials`), never executed.
- [ ] A second message resumes the same session id, and the model demonstrates memory of the
      first turn.
- [ ] Killing a session mid-turn (SIGTERM) and resuming with a steering message continues with
      full memory.
- [ ] A resume against a deleted transcript fails loud and a fresh session starts, seeded from
      the card, with the new id persisted (exercised on a card-attached kind, `refine` on a
      story or `define` on an epic; shaping threads gain id persistence in 001-04).
- [ ] Two kinds spawn with visibly different registry rows (`shape` on Fable read-only,
      `research` on Sonnet) with no per-call configuration.

## Out of scope

- The MCP board tools and `--mcp-config` wiring (001-02).
- Any widget or chat UI (001-03).
- Run-kind compaction, the permission-prompt tool, and hooks (roadmap step 2).
- The queue: sessions here spawn directly on demand (001-06 adds the dispatcher).

## Open questions

None.

You are Helm's review session for story 001-01, spec axis. You are a cold reader: no chat history, no contact with the implementing agent. Your product is evidence.

The story branch `helm/001-01-session-runner` is checked out (cwd). The diff under review is `git diff master...HEAD`. The brief the run was spawned with follows at the end of this prompt; grade the diff against its Acceptance criteria section, nothing else.

For each acceptance criterion output:
- **Verdict**: ✓ (met), ✗ (not met), or unclear.
- **Evidence**: file:line references, and output of any command you actually ran.
- **Checkbox**: mark `[x]` only when a command you ran in this session proves the criterion. A criterion graded from code inspection alone stays `[ ]` and gets a "human check" note with exact steps to reproduce and verify it by hand.

You have read-only tools plus Bash limited to read-only git and `pnpm check` (the repo's test command; there are no tests in this repo by policy). You cannot write files or spawn sessions, so runtime criteria will likely rest on code inspection: trace the code paths carefully and say precisely what the code does and does not guarantee.

Also grade the Blast radius and Out of scope sections: flag anything the diff touches outside the declared blast radius, and anything in-scope that was deferred.

End with exactly one line: `TALLY: <n>/<total> met, <n> unclear, <n> not met`.

- **`08e1eeb feat(sessions): session runner and kind registry`**
  - `src/sessions/kinds.ts` — the registry as data: one row per kind (all nine from `session-kinds.md`) with model, effort, context policy, and, on the wired kinds (`init`, `shape`, `define`, `refine`, `research`, `adversary`), the read-only tool allowlist and system-prompt contract. `run`/`review`/`conflict` rows carry their settled model/effort/context but are not spawnable until their mechanics (permission presets, test command, worktrees) land; spawning one throws.
  - `src/sessions/runner.ts` — spawns `claude -p` with `--output-format stream-json --verbose --include-partial-messages --model --effort --permission-mode default --allowedTools --append-system-prompt --strict-mcp-config`, parses the NDJSON stream through a tolerant zod boundary, exposes `started` (resolves on `system/init`), `done` (exit outcome with stale detection), and `kill()` (SIGTERM). Strips `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`/`ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN` and the nested-session markers from the child env while keeping `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`.
  - `src/sessions/events.ts` / `prompts.ts` — event schemas plus the reseed and steering prompt builders.
  - `src/shared/channels.ts` — new `session` WS channel: `event` (every CLI event, tagged `{runId, kind, sessionId}`) and `closed` (`exitCode`, `signal`, `stale`).
  - `src/server/services/sessions.ts` — wires runner to WS; tracks live/known/interrupted sessions; reseeds stale card-attached sessions; persists ids.
  - `src/worker/routes/session.ts` — `session.spawn` / `session.message` / `session.kill`.
  - `src/board/store.ts` — `attachStorySession` / `attachEpicSession` frontmatter writes; the write queue moved from `routes/story.ts` to `src/server/write-queue.ts` so moves and session attaches serialize through one writer.
- **`cddddae docs`** — `claude-integration.md` (effort flag verified, partial messages, explicit permission mode), `session-kinds.md` (effort no longer unverified), `api.md` (procedures, channel, error codes), `CLAUDE.md` module map.

## Per-criterion verification

All five criteria were exercised end-to-end against the real CLI (2.1.177) by a throwaway harness (deleted before commit) that booted the actual node server with a toy managed repo, subscribed to `/ws`, and drove the RPC procedures. Observed output:

1. **Shape spawn, model, WS streaming, denial** ✅ — init over WS: `model=claude-fable-5 permissionMode=default`; events streamed as they arrived (21 `stream_event` partials); `permission_denials: [{"tool_name":"Bash","tool_input":{"command":"touch denied.txt"...}}]` and `denied.txt` was never created.
2. **Resume with memory** ✅ — `session.message` returned the same id; the model replied `"done"`, the word from turn one.
3. **Kill mid-turn + steering** ✅ — SIGTERM mid-generation: `closed: exitCode=143 signal=null`, no `result` event; the steering resume kept the id and the model described the interrupted counting task (and, responding to the auto-prepended steering preamble, noted no partial side effects).
4. **Stale reseed with persistence** ✅ — `refine` spawn persisted `sessions: { refine: b3868c27-… }` in story frontmatter; after deleting the transcript, the resume failed loud (`closed` frame with `stale: true`, exit 1) and a fresh session answered *"This story is about shipping a blue widget, carved from blue stone, that spins…"* (seeded from the card), with the new id `907a7d94-…` replacing the old in frontmatter.
5. **Two kinds, different registry rows** ✅ — `research` spawn with only `{kind, prompt}` reported `model=claude-sonnet-4-6` vs shape's Fable; bonus check: messaging the research session was rejected with `SESSION_COLD` (always-cold policy).

## Decisions taken (no user available)

- **Effort mechanism**: current CLI has a first-class `--effort` flag (valid: low/medium/high/xhigh/max; invalid values warn and fall back). Recorded in the knowledge base; the older "settings key?" open question is resolved.
- **Explicit `--permission-mode default` on every spawn**: the user's own config defaults headless spawns to `auto`, which would auto-approve tools outside the allowlist. Pinning the mode is what makes the denial criterion hold; documented in `claude-integration.md`.
- **Unspawnable rows for `run`/`review`/`conflict`**: their allowlists can't be expressed yet without inventing (presets, repo test command, worktree tools), so the rows exist as data but spawn throws. This matches the brief's "only the chat kinds and the two cold kinds need entries wired to behavior".
- **Turn lifecycle contract**: `spawn`/`message` return at `system/init`; the session stays `SESSION_BUSY` until its `closed` WS frame (documented in `api.md`). 001-03's chat UI should treat `closed`, not `result`, as end-of-turn.
- **Steering preamble is automatic**: a session whose last process ended without a `result` event is marked interrupted, and the next resume gets the "your turn was interrupted" preamble prepended server-side.
- **Write-queue move**: a small refactor beyond the listed blast radius, needed so the new frontmatter writes share the existing serialization instead of a parallel queue.

## For a human to verify by hand

- Nothing functional is outstanding. Two notes: the local CLI is **2.1.177** while the earlier spike README records 2.1.210 (possibly a downgrade/rollback on this machine); all flags were re-verified against the installed binary. And re-running the harness needs a `helm.config.json` pointing at a scratch repo with one epic/story (I used `/tmp/helm-toy`, left in place).

You are a Helm implementation run for story 001-01 (Session runner & kind registry). Your prompt is the story brief; it is the contract you deliver against. You work in a dedicated git worktree on branch `helm/001-01-session-runner` (cwd). The repo's CLAUDE.md rules apply in full, including the playbook pull rules.

Run rules:
- Deliver every acceptance criterion. Never narrow scope silently; if something is truly undeliverable, say exactly what and why in your final report.
- No user is available mid-run. The board tools (`update_card`, `ask_user`) do not exist yet, so make reasonable decisions yourself and record each decision and any open question in your final report.
- Verify for real. The acceptance criteria describe runtime behavior against the actual `claude` CLI; a small throwaway debug script (per the repo's spike conventions, or a temporary script you delete before committing) exercising your module is the expected way to check them. Keep verification prompts tiny and cheap ("Reply with one word") so they cost near-zero tokens. The `claude` CLI is on PATH and authenticated.
- Stay on this branch. Never push, never switch branches, never touch anything outside the worktree. `.helm/board/` is deliberately absent from your checkout; board writes are the orchestrator's job, not yours.
- `pnpm check` must be green before you finish.
- Commit your work on this branch with Conventional Commits (header ≤ ~60 chars, body says the why). Multiple commits are fine.
- End with a final report: what you built, per-criterion verification status with the evidence (command + observed output), decisions taken, and anything a human must verify by hand.
